originalTitle: Wagner: Der Ring des Nibelungen album review – Luisi has a keen sense of the operatic architecture
summary: Captured live in concert performances, Fabio Luisi’s clear-sighted command and strong orchestral playing make this Wagner set frequently impressive, with Mark Delavan an authorative Wotan
content: Concert performances of opera can provide ideal conditions for live recordings. This ambitious release of Wagner’s Ring Cycle on 13 CDs, captured in 2024 with the Dallas Symphony under music director Fabio Luisi, is a fine example. The Italian maestro has a strong record, having stepped in at the Metropolitan Opera in 2011 when James Levine had to withdraw from Siegfried due to illness. With his clearheaded approach, a keen sense of Wagner’s operatic architecture, and a supple way with phrasing, he is perhaps the most compelling reason for acquiring this frequently impressive set. Of course, any Ring lives or dies on its singers, and no cast will ever be perfect. As Wotan, Mark Delavan’s voice carries the right authority, his characterisation intensifying as the work unfolds. Daniel Johansson is a lyrical Siegfried, never straining, even if he occasionally sounds uninvolved. As Brünnhilde, Lise Lindstrom’s soprano comes under rather too much pressure, though she’s never less than committed to text and drama. Among the rest, Sara Jakubiak stands out as a radiant Sieglinde, though a tired-sounding Christopher Ventris disappoints as Siegmund. Stephen Milling, Štefan Margita and Michael Laurenz sing and act their socks off as Hagen, Loge and Mime respectively, with Tómas Tómasson characterful but occasionally wobbly as Alberich. If Deniz Uzun is rather soft-edged as Fricka and Roman Trekel overly gravelly as Gunther, Kathryn Henry offers a glorious Gutrune and Tamara Mumford an imposing Erda. Giants and minor deities are good; Rhinemaidens, Valkyries, and especially Norns excellent. Sound is admirable, if slightly more congested than the finest studio sets. Listen on Apple Music (above) or Spotify
chineseTitle: 瓦格纳：《尼伯龙根的指环》专辑评论——路易斯对歌剧结构有着敏锐的感觉
chineseSummary: 法比奥·路易斯 (Fabio Luisi) 清晰的指挥和强有力的管弦乐演奏在音乐会表演中的现场捕捉，使这套瓦格纳的布景经常令人印象深刻，而马克·德拉万 (Mark Delavan) 则是一位权威的沃坦
chineseContent: 歌剧音乐会演出可以为现场录音提供理想的条件。瓦格纳的《指环循环》雄心勃勃地发行了 13 张 CD，由音乐总监法比奥·路易斯 (Fabio Luisi) 领导的达拉斯交响乐团于 2024 年录制，就是一个很好的例子。这位意大利大师有着辉煌的业绩，2011年詹姆斯·莱文因病不得不退出《齐格弗里德》时，他就介入了大都会歌剧院。凭借他头脑清醒的方法、对瓦格纳歌剧结构的敏锐感觉以及灵活的措辞方式，他也许是购买这套令人印象深刻的作品的最令人信服的原因。当然，《指环王》的生死存亡都取决于歌手，而且没有哪个演员是完美的。作为沃坦，马克·德拉文的声音具有正确的权威，随着作品的展开，他的人物塑造也愈发强化。丹尼尔·约翰逊是抒情的齐格弗里德，从不紧张，即使他偶尔听起来漫不经心。莉丝·林德斯特罗姆饰演的女高音布伦希尔德承受着相当大的压力，尽管她对文本和戏剧的投入从未减少。在其他人中，萨拉·雅库比亚克（Sara Jakubiak）饰演光芒四射的齐格琳德（Sieglinde）脱颖而出，尽管克里斯托弗·文特里斯（Christopher Ventris）饰演的齐格蒙德（Siegmund）听起来很疲倦，但令人失望。斯蒂芬·米林 (Stephen Milling)、斯特凡·玛吉塔 (Štefan Margita) 和迈克尔·劳伦兹 (Michael Laurenz) 分别饰演哈根 (Hagen)、洛格 (Loge) 和哑剧 (Mime)，托马斯·托马森 (Tómas Tómasson) 饰演阿尔贝里奇 (Alberich)，性格鲜明，但偶尔摇摆不定。如果说德尼兹·乌尊（Deniz Uzun）像弗里卡（Fricka）那样边缘柔和，而罗曼·特雷克尔（Roman Trekel）像冈瑟（Gunther）那样过于沙哑，那么凯瑟琳·亨利（Kathryn Henry）则提供了辉煌的古特鲁恩（Gutrune）和塔玛拉·芒福德（Tamara Mumford）令人印象深刻的埃尔达（Erda）。巨人和小神都很好；莱茵少女、女武神，尤其是诺恩，都非常出色。声音令人钦佩，尽管比最好的录音室布景稍微拥挤一些。在 Apple Music（上图）或 Spotify 上收听
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
